skill.name: dead-code-detector
skill.description: Find unused imports, variables, functions, dependencies, and unreachable code so teams can remove dead code safely.
skill.summary: Detect dead code and organize removal work by confidence and review needs.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, network, and weak cryptography patterns, but manual review found they are documentation examples or keyword matches in SKILL.md. No executable code, network call, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives. The cited locations are Markdown fenced examples and inline code samples that describe unused imports, variables, report output, and edge cases; they do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the locations are inside SKILL.md documentation blocks. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command runner is present in the skill.

low_findings.title: Network Library Mention Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The network finding at line 147 is a false positive. The word axios appears in an example list of unused npm packages and is not used to make an HTTP request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears under an example report section about dependency cleanup. No HTTP client code or external endpoint is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Match Is a Keyword False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding at line 3 is a false positive. The line is the skill description and contains no cryptographic operation or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML front matter describing dead-code detection. There is no DES, MD5, SHA-1, or other cryptographic implementation in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Unused code and dependencies increase maintenance cost and review noise. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code identify likely dead code and separate safe removals from review items.
actual_capabilities: Identifies unused imports, variables, functions, methods, and type definitions.
Separates findings into safe removal, potentially unused, and review-required groups.
Highlights unreachable code paths, commented code blocks, and deprecated code still present.
Reviews dependency usage for unused packages and dependency classification issues.
Calls out dynamic references, test-only usage, and public APIs that need human review.
limitations: Cannot prove every exported symbol is unused outside the current repository.
May miss code referenced through reflection, strings, generated code, or runtime configuration.
Requires project tests or coverage data to increase confidence for removal decisions.
Does not automatically delete code unless the user separately requests edits.

use_cases.title: Clean a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Find unused imports, variables, and helper functions before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Reduce Package Bloat
use_cases.description: Identify dependencies that are installed but no longer referenced by application code.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Legacy Module Review
use_cases.description: Create a review list for stale exports, commented blocks, and unreachable branches.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Dead Code Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dead-code-detector on src/. Report unused imports, variables, and functions. Group findings by safe removal and review required.

prompt_templates.title: Dependency Cleanup Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dead-code-detector to compare package dependencies with actual imports. List unused packages and dependency classification issues.

prompt_templates.title: Conservative Public API Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dead-code-detector on this library. Treat exported symbols as review required unless tests, examples, and documentation show no usage.

prompt_templates.title: Removal Plan With Risk Levels
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dead-code-detector across the repository. Produce a removal plan with confidence levels, test commands, and separate commits for each cleanup group.

output_examples.input: Scan a React application for unused code before a pull request.
output: Summary of unused imports, variables, functions, and commented blocks.
A safe-to-remove section for items with clear local evidence.
A review-required section for exports, dynamic references, and test helpers.

output_examples.input: Check package dependencies for cleanup opportunities.
output: List of packages that appear unused by application imports.
List of dependencies that may belong in development dependencies.
Recommended verification steps before uninstalling packages.

output_examples.input: Audit an old service module for unreachable branches.
output: Locations of code after returns or impossible conditions.
Notes about branches that may depend on runtime configuration.
A cautious cleanup sequence with tests after each removal.

best_practices: Run tests after each cleanup group, especially when removing exported code.
Search documentation, examples, and configuration for dynamic references before deletion.
Keep dependency removals and code removals in separate commits for easier review.
anti_patterns: Removing public exports without checking downstream consumers or versioning impact.
Treating low-confidence dynamic references as safe removals.
Combining large cleanup edits with feature work in the same pull request.

faq.question: Does this skill delete code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It produces findings and removal guidance unless the user separately asks for edits.

faq.question: Can it detect unused dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes. It reviews installed packages against imports and usage patterns, then recommends verification before removal.

faq.question: How does it handle public APIs?
faq.answer: It marks exported symbols as review required when they may be used by external consumers.

faq.question: Can it find dynamic usage?
faq.answer: It can flag likely dynamic references, but runtime behavior may require tests or manual review.

faq.question: Which languages are covered?
faq.answer: The guidance names JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, and Rust tools and patterns.

faq.question: Is this a security scanning skill?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on code cleanup, dependency hygiene, and safe removal planning.
